sn.s: Lucía quiere presentarme a su familia.
sn.e: Lucía wants to introduce me to her family.

sn.s: "Espero que les caigas bien," me dice.
sn.e: "I hope they like you," she tells me.

sn.s: Llego con flores para su mamá.
sn.e: I arrive with flowers for her mom.

sn.s: "Mucho gusto, doña. Lucía me habla mucho de usted."
sn.e: "Nice to meet you, ma'am. Lucía talks about you a lot."

sn.s: "Qué detalle tan lindo," dice su mamá.
sn.e: "What a lovely gesture," her mom says.

sn.s: Su papá me da la mano con firmeza.
sn.e: Her dad shakes my hand firmly.

sn.s: "Así que vos sos el que la hace feliz," dice.
sn.e: "So you're the one who makes her happy," he says.

sn.s: "Hago todo lo posible," respondo con respeto.
sn.e: "I do everything I can," I respond respectfully.

sn.s: Comemos juntos y la plática fluye.
sn.e: We eat together and the conversation flows.
I want to make a good impression.

sn.s: "Espero que se sienta como en casa," dice la mamá.
sn.e: "I hope you feel at home," the mom says.

sn.s: "Ya me siento así. Gracias por recibirme."
sn.e: "I already do. Thank you for having me."

sn.s: Al final, Lucía me susurra: "Les encantaste."
sn.e: At the end, Lucía whispers: "They loved you."

sn.s: Ser aceptado por su familia lo es todo.
sn.e: Being accepted by her family means everything.
